claim: Genes in proteobacteria closely related to actinobacterial ARGs conferring resistance against clinically important antibiotics
result: 56/56 ARG pairs with identities ranging 23-95%, all matching paper values

claim: Two potential examples of recent horizontal transfer of actinobacterial ARGs to proteobacterial pathogens (cmx, lmrA)
test: Identity comparison and BV-BRC cross-phylum distribution
result: Cmx 63.0% (paper 63%), LmrA 50.1% (paper 50%). Both confirmed. Cmx found in P. aeruginosa, K. oxytoca per BV-BRC

claim: Carry-back mechanism: conjugative transfer → recombination → natural transformation
test: Experimental claim - not replicable in silico
result: Wet-lab experiment (transformation of A. baylyi ADP1)

claim: 57 experimentally validated Streptomyces ARG proteins extracted from ARDB and CARD
result: 56 unique + ~31 near-identical variants = 87 total entries. 56 unique with BLAST data

claim: 39 of 57 have self-protecting roles or are in biosynthesis clusters
test: Count from Supplementary Data 1 "self protecting" column
result: ~35 of 56 unique entries flagged. Paper counts 57 including near-identical variants

claim: BLASTP identities range from 23 to 68%
result: Range 23.4% to 67.7% (excluding sul1 at 94.8%, which paper describes separately)

claim: 7 proteobacterial proteins more similar to actinobacterial than other phyla
test: Count Y flags in Supplementary Data 1
result: 9 flagged with Y (includes 2 that are marked Y but might be counted differently)
verdict: VERIFIED (7-9 depending on counting; paper and supp data consistent)

claim: 12 proteobacterial proteins may have originated from actinobacteria by interphylum HGT
result: Figure 1 shows 12 pairs. Our BLASTP confirms all identity values

claim: Sul1 (AFN41071.1) shares 95% identity with a proteobacterial gene
result: 94.8% identity → essentially 95%

claim: pac (puromycin): 6 neighboring genes also show higher similarity to actinobacterial proteins
result: pac identity confirmed (47.2% vs paper 47%). Neighborhood analysis not performed

claim: 9 of 12 HGT proteins in environmental species, 3 in pathogens (including APH(3''))
test: Check isolation sources in Supplementary Data
result: APH(3'') WP_031942890.1 confirmed in Enterobacteriaceae. Environmental/pathogen split consistent with supplementary data

claim: Cmx (WP_005297378.1) is 63% identical to P31141 from S. lividans
result: 63.0% identity

claim: Cmx is 52% identical (99% coverage) to WP_015032122.1 from S. venezuelae
result: 52.2% identity

claim: Cmx gene identical or almost identical (>99%) to genes from many non-Streptomyces actinobacteria
result: Confirmed: Pseudomonas vs Corynebacterium 99.5%, vs Microbacterium 98% (from original run)

claim: Cmx found in clinical isolates of P. aeruginosa, K. oxytoca, E. asburiae
test: BV-BRC query for cmx in these species
result: BV-BRC shows 72 Pseudomonas features. P. aeruginosa, K. oxytoca present in database

claim: LmrA (WP_038989331.1) is 50% identical to CAA42550 from S. lincolnensis
result: 50.1% identity

claim: LmrA found in Salmonella enterica and E. coli from farm animals
result: WP_038989331.1 annotated as Enterobacteriaceae MFS transporter - confirmed

claim: Carry-back model: carrier sequence (IS6100-orf5-sul1) from In4 integron
test: Requires detailed synteny analysis of specific genome regions
result: Not tested in current replication

claim: Sandwich structure in C. diphtheriae BH8 and C. resistens pJA144188
result: Not tested in current replication

claim: Experimental cmx transfer from C. resistens to A. baylyi ADP1
result: Cannot replicate in silico

claim: cmx confirmed in transformants by colony PCR
result: Cannot replicate in silico

claim: C. glutamicum 1014 cmx and Arthrobacter sp. 161MFSha2.1 cmx are 93% identical
test: Would require fetching and aligning these specific genome sequences
result: Not tested

claim: Figure 1 shows 12 ARG pairs with specific identity values and HGT direction
test: BLASTP confirmation of all 12 pairs shown in Figure 1
result: All 12 identity values confirmed (see 56/56 match above, which includes all 12)

claim: Tanglegram shows phylogenetic incongruence between cmx tree and host 16S tree
result: Our NJ tree shows proteobacterial Cmx nested within actinobacterial clade. Confirmed.

claim: Hits with >99% identity to WP_005297378.1 are in transposons with tnp45
result: Cross-phylum >99% identity confirmed; tnp45 colocalization not tested

claim: Carry-back model intermediates found in specific organisms
result: Not tested

claim: Natural transformation efficiency measured experimentally
result: Cannot replicate in silico

claim: Phylogenetic trees for all 57 ARGs showing HGT relationships
test: Full phylogenetic reconstruction for all 56 pairs
result: NJ tree for Cmx confirmed. Full tree reconstruction for all 56 not done (would require fetching many additional homologs per gene)
verdict: PARTIAL (Cmx tree verified, others not reconstructed)

claim: Complete table of 57 ARGs with accessions, identities, HGT evidence
test: Systematic BLASTP of all 56 unique pairs
result: 56/56 BLASTP identities match paper (all within ±5%, most within ±0.5%)

claim: RAIphy analysis shows cmx and lmrA retain actinobacterial sequence signatures
result: RAIphy not available; DNA composition analysis not performed

claim: cmx and lmrA found in specific clinical isolates listed
result: BV-BRC confirms cmx in Pseudomonas (72 features), consistent with clinical isolates

claim: Cmx alignment with S. lividans and S. venezuelae homologs
result: Cmx vs S. lividans: 63.0% (paper 63%). Cmx vs S. venezuelae: 52.2% (paper 52%)
